title: Candidate pairwise query-conditioned token stats MLP

queries: learned token statistics scorer candidate ranking non autoregressive multiclass
query conditioned token interaction MLP scorer scalable candidate ranking

evidence.why_now: EXP150 provided the first clean scientific verdict for the token-stats family and showed that fixed token-statistics residuals are still too weak even under safe GPU placement. The remaining justified step is to keep the same token-level statistics but let the model learn how to weight them through a small scorer, rather than relying on a hand-designed residual combination.

hypothesis: If the token-level candidate evidence is useful but the fixed residual in EXP150 is too rigid, then replacing that hand-designed combination with a learned token-statistics scorer should recover more salt-side signal while keeping the multiclass path non-autoregressive and Stage 4 compatible.
notes: Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not introduce pretrain, loss-family changes, sampling changes, or packaging changes.
This family keeps the safe-GPU serial placement and the same query-conditioned token statistics as EXP150, but replaces the fixed residual weights with a learned token-statistics scorer.
Use serial_batch_equivalent scheduling with explicit task_gpus so all three representative tasks run on the safer GPU 5,6 pair.
Launched on 2026-05-09 through the local Stage 3 launcher.
Completed on 2026-05-09 as implementation-blocked rather than scientifically resolved.
EXP153 finished user-churn clearly better with roc_auc=0.6882353860642509 against strict baseline 0.6540209032382359.
EXP154 finished user-ltv neutral under replay-aware effective delta with mae=79.65295739190421 against strict baseline 79.27257269903086 and noise-floor delta 6.511244718243262.
EXP155 failed before the first evaluation with ValueError: Query-conditioned token stats require query/key projections.

Root cause: the token-stats MLP interface was included in the inner query/key projection creation branch but omitted from the outer initialization guard, so item-incoterms could not construct the required projections.
Do not use EXP153/154/155 as scientific evidence against the learned token-stats MLP family; rerun cleanly after the projection-init fix.
